description: Lock Surgical Advances (Pare) to Paré's gunshot-wound, ligature, amputation, and campaign-surgery innovations, not general metallurgy.

checks.reason: Ligature, amputation, wound treatment, and prosthetic work require practical anatomical knowledge.
Anatomy enables the surgery without being the whole Paré innovation.
The 1545 treatment method is explicitly about arquebus and firearm wounds.
Early firearms provide the historical injury context, not a surgical component.
Paré's innovations came through campaign surgery and battlefield wound care.
Military field medicine is the historical practice lineage for Paré's campaign surgery.
General medieval metallurgy should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to metal instrument or prosthesis fabrication.
The node remains anchored to Paré's 1545 firearm-wound treatment publication.
